assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies the issue as cert-manager webhook certificates being invalid for the new API server after the control plane upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides exact commands: kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager and kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Offers Option A to temporarily disable webhook by setting failurePolicy to Ignore and Option B to restart cert-manager

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Does not provide kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration itself, only mentions deleting the webhook secret

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly warn about removing validation through webhook deletion, though it does mention temporarily weakening security posture

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Does not mention checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Does not advise upgrading cert-manager version, focuses on certificate regeneration instead

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Explicitly shows how to set failurePolicy to Ignore as Option A and positions it as an alternative approach

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification command: kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --rm -it --restart=Never -- echo 'webhook test'

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention persisting changes in source of truth like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Shows how to revert failurePolicy back to Fail after the permanent fix and mentions temporarily weakening security posture
